name: architecture-documenter
description: Document system architecture and technical design decisions for effective team communication and ...
capabilities: Analyzes codebase structure to identify components, services, and dependencies
Generates architecture diagrams showing component relationships and data flows
Documents design decisions with context, alternatives considered, and trade-offs
Creates data flow diagrams showing how information moves through the system
Produces onboarding guides for new team members
Maintains architecture decision logs for audit and traceability
author: CuriousLearner
securitySummary: Static analysis detected pattern matches in documentation text that appear to be false positives. All flagged terms (weak crypto, keychain/SAM access, C2 keywords) are references within documentation content, not executable code. No actual security vulnerabilities found in the skill's implementation. This is a documentation-only skill with no network or file system access.
valueStatement: Projects often lack clear architecture documentation, making onboarding difficult and decisions hard to trace. The architecture-documenter skill generates comprehensive documentation including component diagrams, data flows, and design decision logs from codebase analysis.
limitations: Cannot access private repositories or external systems not present in the codebase
Cannot execute code or run the application to verify runtime behavior
Cannot generate deployment or infrastructure diagrams without explicit configuration

useCases.targetUser: Engineering managers and team leads onboarding new developers
useCases.title: New Team Member Onboarding
useCases.description: Generate comprehensive architecture documentation to help new developers understand system structure, component interactions, and key design decisions quickly.

useCases.title: Technical Documentation Maintenance
useCases.description: Create up-to-date architecture documents after significant refactoring or feature additions to keep documentation synchronized with implementation.

useCases.targetUser: Senior engineers and architects documenting decision rationale
useCases.title: Architecture Decision Recording
useCases.description: Document architectural decisions with context, alternatives evaluated, and trade-offs made to create an authoritative decision log for future reference.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Architecture Overview
promptTemplates.prompt: Document the architecture of this codebase. Include the main components, their responsibilities, and how they interact with each other. Describe the data flow through the system.
promptTemplates.scenario: Getting a high-level understanding of a new codebase

promptTemplates.title: Component Deep Dive
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the [COMPONENT_NAME] component in detail. Document its internal structure, dependencies, interfaces, and how it integrates with other parts of the system.
promptTemplates.scenario: Understanding a specific module or service

promptTemplates.title: Design Decision Log
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an architecture decision record (ADR) for the major design decisions in this project. Include the context, the decision made, alternatives considered, and the trade-offs involved.
promptTemplates.scenario: Recording architectural decisions with full context

promptTemplates.title: Complete System Documentation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate comprehensive documentation for this system including: 1) Executive summary of the architecture, 2) Component diagram description, 3) Data flow diagrams, 4) Technology stack overview, 5) Design decision log, and 6) Integration points with external systems.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating complete system documentation for stakeholders

bestPractices: Provide context about the system scope and boundaries before requesting documentation
Specify the target audience (technical vs. non-technical) for appropriate detail level
Review generated documentation against actual implementation for accuracy
antiPatterns: Asking for documentation of a codebase without first understanding its scope
Requesting architecture diagrams without specifying the level of abstraction
Using generated documentation without validating against actual code behavior

faq.question: What types of architecture documentation can this skill generate?
faq.answer: The skill can generate component diagrams, data flow diagrams, architecture decision records, system overview documents, technology stack documentation, and integration guides. Output formats include Markdown, Mermaid diagrams, and structured text.

faq.question: Can this skill document any programming language or framework?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill analyzes source code structure, patterns, and relationships regardless of language. It works with any language where code structure can be parsed, including JavaScript, Python, Java, Go, Rust, and Ruby.

faq.question: How detailed are the generated diagrams?
faq.answer: Detail level depends on your prompt. You can request high-level architecture overviews or deep-dive component analysis. The skill will identify modules, services, APIs, and data stores based on code patterns.

faq.question: Does this skill access external systems or make network requests?
faq.answer: No. The skill only analyzes code present in the project. It cannot access external APIs, databases, or services. All documentation is generated from static code analysis.

faq.question: Can I use this for legacy or undocumented codebases?
faq.answer: Yes. This is particularly valuable for legacy systems where documentation may be outdated or missing. The skill can reverse-engineer architecture from code structure and patterns.

faq.question: How does this handle microservices or distributed systems?
faq.answer: The skill identifies service boundaries through API patterns, communication protocols, and dependency analysis. It can document inter-service communication, message queues, and API contracts.
